title: Vibrant Silky Peruvian Fish Ceviche Clasico
description: A funky and adventurous Peruvian dish featuring dark soy sauce and steam, this Jain-friendly ceviche is a unique twist on a classic recipe, with a silky texture that will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Cut the halibut fillets into small cubes and place them in a heatproof bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dark soy sauce and lime juice until well combined.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the fish cubes, making sure they are completely covered.
Add the mixed citrus segments, red onion, aji amarillo peppers, and chopped cilantro to the bowl with the fish.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and place it over a pot of steaming water, making sure the bowl is not touching the water.
Steam the ceviche for 5 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and opaque.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in the salt.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap again and refrigerate the ceviche for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, give the ceviche a good stir and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
